name: Qi Lan
terroir: Originally from Anxi; introduced to Wuyi in the 1930s. Adapts well to Wuyi rocky terroir, gaining mineral depth absent in Anxi-grown versions
cultivar: Qi Lan (vigorous grower, widely planted across southern Fujian and Guangdong)
flavor_profile: Lives up to its name — orchid aromatics are deep and persistent, supported by almond blossom and a warm baked-grain quality. The liquor is golden-amber with a creamy, coating mouthfeel. Honeyed mineral sweetness runs underneath, and the Wuyi terroir adds the characteristic rock rhyme that separates it from Anxi Qi Lan. Forgiving to brew and rewarding across many steeps.

brewing.rinse_hint: Quick rinse — Qi Lan opens easily and the first real steep is ready fast.
brewing.tips: One of the more forgiving yancha to brew. Works well with both short flash steeps and slightly longer infusions. The orchid character is most pronounced in steeps 2-4.
